task_completion.description: Create a new style named 'heatmap_population' in the 'ne' workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoServer web interface (Styles page), there should be a style listed with the name 'heatmap_population' and the workspace 'ne'.

task_completion.description: Configure the style to use the 'vec:Heatmap' rendering transformation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoServer style editor (SLD code), look for the XML tag <ogc:Function name="vec:Heatmap"> or equivalent rendering transformation configuration.

task_completion.description: Set the transformation parameters: 'pop_max' weight attribute, radius between 15 and 60, and FLOAT output.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the style editor SLD code, look for parameters setting 'weightAttr' to 'pop_max', 'radiusPixels' to a value between 15 and 60, and 'outputFormat' to 'FLOAT' (or similar parameters within the vec:Heatmap function block).

task_completion.description: Define a color map gradient with at least 4 colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the style editor SLD code, look for a <ColorMap> block containing at least 4 distinct <ColorMapEntry> tags with different color values.

task_completion.description: Apply the heatmap style to the 'ne:ne_populated_places' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoServer Layer Publishing tab for 'ne_populated_places', 'heatmap_population' should be selected as the Default Style or an Available Style, OR the Layer Preview should visually show the heatmap applied to the layer.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting heatmap as an 800x400 PNG image covering the world bounds to /home/ga/heatmap_output.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the file '/home/ga/heatmap_output.png' being saved (e.g., via curl/wget) with a WMS URL containing 'width=800', 'height=400', 'bbox=-180,-90,180,90', 'format=image/png', OR an image viewer showing the exported heatmap with these dimensions.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoServer web interface to configure the style and layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the GeoServer web administration GUI (http://localhost:8080/geoserver/web/) rather than exclusively using terminal commands or REST APIs to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The exported image was generated via a genuine GeoServer WMS request.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or browser history should show a WMS GetMap request to the local GeoServer instance (localhost:8080) to download the image, rather than copying a pre-existing file or downloading an image from the external internet.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script (like matplotlib/seaborn) to generate a fake heatmap.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of Python scripts being written or executed to generate the 'heatmap_output.png' file locally bypassing GeoServer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected style name is 'heatmap_population' in the 'ne' workspace. The layer to be styled is 'ne:ne_populated_places'. The rendering transformation must be 'vec:Heatmap' using the 'pop_max' attribute for weight. The color map must have at least 4 colors. The final output must be saved to '/home/ga/heatmap_output.png' and should be a valid PNG image (minimum 5000 bytes) with dimensions 800x400 and bounding box -180,-90,180,90.
